answers.value: Removal of an approximately 20-year-old, deteriorated playground structure and installation of new, Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA)-compliant play equipment, resilient rubberized safety surfacing, accessible pathways, and site furnishings within the existing developed footprint of Northgate Park.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: The purpose of the project is to replace aging, non-compliant equipment to enhance safety, improve accessibility for children and caregivers with disabilities, and extend the useful life of an existing neighborhood park facility serving residents of the Northgate and Gardenland neighborhoods, nearby schools, and childcare providers.

answers.value: Class 1, Existing Facilities (CEQA Guidelines §15301); Class 2, Replacement or Reconstruction (CEQA Guidelines §15302); and Class 3, New Construction or Conversion of Small Structures (CEQA Guidelines §15303)

answers.value: The project consists of minor alterations and replacement of existing recreational facilities within an existing, fully developed public park, involving negligible expansion of use beyond that existing, and is therefore exempt under CEQA Guidelines Sections 15301 (Existing Facilities), 15302 (Replacement or Reconstruction), and 15303 (New Construction or Conversion of Small Structures).
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: All work will occur within the existing disturbed park footprint, no increase in park capacity or change in use is proposed, and there is no evidence of unusual circumstances, sensitive biological or cultural resources, or other factors that would trigger an exception to the categorical exemptions under CEQA Guidelines Section 15300.2.
